content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a game using a string SSS of length NNN. They both have their individual strings which are initially empty.

Both players take alternate turns. Alice starts first.

In Alice's turn, she will:


Choose a prefix of SSS;
Remove the chosen prefix from SSS;
Append the prefix to the end of her string.
In Bob's turn, he will:


Choose a suffix of SSS;
Remove the chosen suffix from SSS;
**Reverse** the suffix and append it to the end of his string.
Chef has decided to reward them if the length of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) of Alice's and Bob's strings is maximized. Help Chef calculate the length of **maximum** LCS that can be achieved by Alice and Bob.

Note:


A prefix is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the end of the string.
A suffix is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the beginning of the string.
**Please use fast I/O for input and pypy for python submissions.**

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines, the first line contains a single integer NNN denoting the length of the original string.
The second line contains the original string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output the length of the maximum LCS achievable by Alice and Bob.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5000
S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string S. The second line contains the string S consisting of N lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the maximum possible length of the Longest Common Subsequence between Alice's and Bob's strings.

solution.approach: The game splits the original string S into a prefix taken by Alice and a suffix taken by Bob (which is reversed before appending). Let i be the length of Alice's prefix and j be the length of Bob's suffix. After the moves, Alice's string is S[0..i-1] and Bob's string is reverse(S[N-j..N-1]). The two strings are subsequences of S and reverse(S) respectively. Therefore the LCS of the two strings is exactly the LCS of the prefix S[0..i-1] and the prefix of reverse(S) of length j. We need the maximum LCS value over all i, j such that i + j <= N (the remaining middle part may be empty). This can be computed by a standard DP for LCS between S and its reverse. While filling the DP table, whenever the current cell (i, j) satisfies i + j <= N we update the answer with dp[i][j]. Because the total N over all test cases is at most 5000, an O(N^2) DP with O(N) memory per test case easily fits the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, O(total N^2) over all test cases (≤ 25·10^6 operations for the given limits).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) additional memory using two rows of the DP table.
